categories.introduction: The Grain & Starch Ontology catalogs canonical culinary grains, pseudocereals, processed grain forms, and starches used worldwide as staple ingredients. This domain models culinary ingredients, not prepared foods or crop science.
categories.name: Grains & Starches
categories.seo_description: Explore canonical culinary grains, pseudocereals, and starches by group — usage intensity, identity, and pairing metadata in the PairingMethod Food Ontology.
categories.summary: Canonical culinary grains and starches across four immutable groups: Whole Grains, Pseudocereals, Processed Grains, and Starches.

groups.introduction: Whole grains in the Grain & Starch Ontology — intact or minimally processed cereal grains used as staple bases, including rice, wheat, barley, rye, oats, corn, millet, and sorghum.
groups.name: Whole Grains
groups.seo_description: Canonical whole culinary grains — rice, wheat, barley, and related staple ingredients in the PairingMethod Grain & Starch Ontology.
groups.summary: Whole Grains — 22 canonical grains and starches in the Grain & Starch Ontology.

groups.introduction: Pseudocereals in the Grain & Starch Ontology — non-cereal seeds used as staple grains in cooking, including quinoa, buckwheat, and amaranth.
groups.name: Pseudocereals
groups.seo_description: Canonical pseudocereals — quinoa, buckwheat, amaranth, and related staple ingredients in the PairingMethod Grain & Starch Ontology.
groups.summary: Pseudocereals — 9 canonical grains and starches in the Grain & Starch Ontology.

groups.introduction: Processed grains in the Grain & Starch Ontology — milled and processed grain forms that function as canonical culinary ingredients, including wheat flour, rice flour, cornmeal, semolina, and bulgur.
groups.name: Processed Grains
groups.seo_description: Canonical processed grain ingredients — wheat flour, rice flour, cornmeal, and related staples in the PairingMethod Grain & Starch Ontology.
groups.summary: Processed Grains — 25 canonical grains and starches in the Grain & Starch Ontology.

groups.introduction: Starches in the Grain & Starch Ontology — isolated culinary starches used as thickeners, coatings, and structural ingredients, including cornstarch, potato starch, and tapioca starch.
groups.name: Starches
groups.seo_description: Canonical culinary starches — cornstarch, potato starch, tapioca starch, and related ingredients in the PairingMethod Grain & Starch Ontology.
groups.summary: Starches — 20 canonical grains and starches in the Grain & Starch Ontology.

meta.description: Grain & Starch Ontology — canonical catalog populated (FOOD-08B). Intrinsic metadata only; relationships deferred to FOOD-08D/E.

aroma_profile.description: Reserved aroma characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-08A; do not populate until post-FOOD-08B curation standards.

canonical_culinary_groups.description: Immutable group names and slugs — do not rename or add peer groups in v1.

culinary_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group value on leaf entities — aligns to immutable group slugs.

flavor_profile.description: Reserved flavor characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-08A; do not populate until post-FOOD-08B curation standards.

texture_profile.description: Reserved texture characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-08A; do not populate until post-FOOD-08B curation standards.

usage_intensity.description: Intrinsic culinary prominence in dish composition — not Pairing Engine reasoning.

domain_exclusions.beverages: Beer, spirits, and fermented grain beverages — out of domain
domain_exclusions.note: Entities in these domains are cross-referenced by canonical ID only — never duplicated intrinsically.
domain_exclusions.prepared_foods: Breads, noodles, pasta, pastries, breakfast cereals — not ingredient ontology

grain_starch.description: Canonical culinary grain, pseudocereal, processed grain, or starch entity with frozen intrinsic metadata.

grain_starch_category.description: Top-level grain & starch domain hub (single category in v1).

grain_starch_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group (Whole Grains, Starches, etc.).

botanical_ownership_rule.note: Suite rule codified in Herb & Spice FOOD-07A — applies when botanical lineage diverges from culinary identity.

processing_ownership_rule.description: When processing creates a distinct canonical culinary ingredient versus a preparation state or alias.

schema.taxonomy_note: Groups represent culinary identity and processing level, not botanical families. See docs/GRAIN_STARCH_GOVERNANCE.md.
